---
title: "Resume"
date-modified: 2026-05-23
title-block-banner: true
toc: true
---

<style>
/* Chip / badge row */
.chips {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 0.35rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.6rem;
}
.chip {
  display: inline-block;
  padding: 0.25rem 0.7rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  font-size: 0.82rem;
  font-weight: 500;
  line-height: 1.4;
}
.chip-outline {
  border: 1.5px solid var(--bs-primary, #0d6efd);
  color: var(--bs-primary, #0d6efd);
}
.chip-filled {
  background: var(--bs-primary, #0d6efd);
  color: #fff;
}
.chip-muted {
  background: var(--bs-secondary, #6c757d);
  color: #fff;
}

/* Vertical timeline */
.timeline {
  position: relative;
  padding-left: 2rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
}
.timeline::before {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  left: 0.44rem;
  top: 0.45rem;
  bottom: 0.45rem;
  width: 2px;
  background: var(--bs-primary, #0d6efd);
  opacity: 0.22;
}
.tl-item {
  position: relative;
  margin-bottom: 1.8rem;
}
.tl-item::before {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  left: -1.63rem;
  top: 0.38rem;
  width: 0.58rem;
  height: 0.58rem;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: var(--bs-primary, #0d6efd);
}
.tl-date {
  font-size: 0.82rem;
  color: #888;
  margin-bottom: 0.15rem;
}
.tl-inst {
  font-style: italic;
}
.tl-title {
  font-weight: 600;
  font-size: 1rem;
  margin: 0.05rem 0 0.4rem;
}
.tl-body {
  font-size: 0.95rem;
}
.tl-body ul {
  margin: 0.3rem 0 0;
  padding-left: 1.15rem;
}
.tl-body li {
  margin-bottom: 0.25rem;
}
</style>

<a href="assets/documents/academic-resume.pdf" class="btn btn-primary mt-1 mb-4" download>
  <i class="bi bi-download me-1"></i> Download PDF Resume
</a>

## Research Interests

<div class="chips">
  <span class="chip chip-outline">Stochastic Games</span>
  <span class="chip chip-outline">Stochastic Differential Equations</span>
  <span class="chip chip-outline">Groundwater Management</span>
  <span class="chip chip-outline">Financial Mathematics</span>
  <span class="chip chip-outline">Deep Learning</span>
</div>

## Skills

**Programming Languages**

<div class="chips">
  <span class="chip chip-filled">Python</span>
  <span class="chip chip-filled">R</span>
  <span class="chip chip-filled">LaTeX</span>
  <span class="chip chip-filled">Markdown</span>
  <span class="chip chip-filled">Git</span>
</div>

**Libraries & Frameworks**

<div class="chips">
  <span class="chip chip-muted">NumPy</span>
  <span class="chip chip-muted">Pandas</span>
  <span class="chip chip-muted">Matplotlib</span>
  <span class="chip chip-muted">Scikit-learn</span>
  <span class="chip chip-muted">PyTorch</span>
  <span class="chip chip-muted">Statsmodels</span>
  <span class="chip chip-muted">SciPy</span>
</div>

**Tools**

<div class="chips">
  <span class="chip chip-muted">Quarto</span>
  <span class="chip chip-muted">Jupyter</span>
  <span class="chip chip-muted">GitHub</span>
  <span class="chip chip-muted">VS Code</span>
</div>

## Education

<div class="timeline">

<div class="tl-item">
<div class="tl-date">2022 — Present &middot; <span class="tl-inst">UC Santa Barbara</span></div>
<div class="tl-title">PhD in Statistics and Applied Probability</div>
<div class="tl-body">
<strong>Committee:</strong> <a href="https://ludkovski.pstat.ucsb.edu">Prof. M. Ludkovski</a> (supervisor),
<a href="https://fouque.faculty.pstat.ucsb.edu">Prof. J.P. Fouque</a>,
<a href="https://sites.google.com/site/ruimenghu1/">Prof. R. Hu</a>.<br>
<strong>Thesis:</strong> Stochastic game models for groundwater markets in California.<br>
<strong>Coursework:</strong> Probability Theory &middot; Statistical Theory &middot; Machine Learning &middot; Energy Finance &middot; Financial Models &middot; Advanced Time Series &middot; Bayesian Analysis &middot; High-Frequency Trading
</div>
</div>

<div class="tl-item">
<div class="tl-date">2020 — 2021 &middot; <span class="tl-inst">Lancaster University</span></div>
<div class="tl-title">MSc Quantitative Finance <em>(Distinction)</em></div>
<div class="tl-body">
<strong>Supervisors:</strong> <a href="https://www.lancaster.ac.uk/lums/people/george-wang">Dr George Wang</a> (Lancaster),
<a href="https://www.stern.nyu.edu/faculty/bio/ian-dsouza">Prof. Ian D'Souza</a> (NYU Stern).<br>
<strong>Dissertation:</strong> Measuring and Forecasting Mutual Fund Survival Capacity using Machine Learning Algorithms.<br>
<strong>Coursework:</strong> Derivatives Pricing &middot; Financial Econometrics &middot; Market Risk Forecasting &middot; Stochastic Calculus &middot; Extreme Value Methods &middot; Forecasting
</div>
</div>

<div class="tl-item">
<div class="tl-date">2013 — 2018 &middot; <span class="tl-inst">Lancaster University</span></div>
<div class="tl-title">BSc Mathematics with Statistics <em>(First Class Honours)</em></div>
<div class="tl-body">
<strong>Coursework:</strong> Real Analysis &middot; Complex Analysis &middot; Linear Algebra &middot; Probability &middot; Statistical Modelling &middot; Bayesian Inference &middot; Time Series Analysis &middot; Differential Equations &middot; Combinatorics
</div>
</div>

</div>

## Employment

<div class="timeline">

<div class="tl-item">
<div class="tl-date">2025 — Present &middot; <span class="tl-inst">UC Santa Barbara</span></div>
<div class="tl-title">Graduate Student Researcher</div>
<div class="tl-body"><ul>
<li>Conducting doctoral research on stochastic game models for groundwater markets under the supervision of Prof. Michael Ludkovski.</li>
<li>Developing deep learning methods for solving high-dimensional stochastic control and mean-field game problems.</li>
<li>Supported by the Amazon Fellowship in Responsible AI.</li>
</ul></div>
</div>

<div class="tl-item">
<div class="tl-date">2022 — Present &middot; <span class="tl-inst">UC Santa Barbara</span></div>
<div class="tl-title">Teaching Assistant / Teaching Associate</div>
<div class="tl-body"><ul>
<li>Led discussion sections, held office hours, and graded assessments across courses in data science, probability, statistics, and survival analysis with combined enrolment of 200+ students per quarter.</li>
<li>Served as instructor of record (Teaching Associate), independently managing all course administration, lectures, and assessments.</li>
</ul></div>
</div>

<div class="tl-item">
<div class="tl-date">2021 — 2022 &middot; <span class="tl-inst">Lancaster University</span></div>
<div class="tl-title">Research Assistant</div>
<div class="tl-body"><ul>
<li>Assisted with research on machine learning methods for mutual fund performance and survival forecasting.</li>
<li>Contributed to data collection, model implementation, and preparation of working paper submitted for publication.</li>
</ul></div>
</div>

</div>

## Fellowships & Grants

- **Amazon Fellowship in Responsible AI** (2024 — Present)
  Awarded by Amazon Science to support doctoral research on responsible AI at the University of California Santa Barbara.

## Working Papers

1. *Measuring and Forecasting Mutual Fund Survival Capacity using Machine Learning Algorithms*, with [Dr George Wang](https://www.lancaster.ac.uk/lums/people/george-wang) (Lancaster University Management School) and [Ian D'Souza](https://www.stern.nyu.edu/faculty/bio/ian-dsouza) (NYU Stern).

## Teaching

| Course | Title | Role |
|--------|-------|------|
| PSTAT 5A | Understanding Data | Teaching Assistant |
| PSTAT 5LS | Life Science Statistics | Teaching Assistant |
| PSTAT 8 | Introduction to Mathematical Proof | Teaching Assistant |
| [PSTAT 10](teaching/pstat10-summer-2025/index.qmd) | Introduction to Data Science | Instructor |
| PSTAT 120C | Probability and Statistics | Teaching Assistant |
| PSTAT 175 | Survival Analysis | Teaching Assistant |
| PSTAT 274 | Time Series Analysis | Teaching Assistant |
| [PSTAT 100](teaching/pstat100-spring-2026/index.qmd) | Data Science Concepts and Analysis | Instructor |
| [PSTAT 160A](teaching/pstat160a-winter-2026/index.qmd) | Stochastic Processes | Teaching Assistant |

## Awards

- **Award for Best Academic Performance 2020/21**
  Highest GPA on the MSc Quantitative Finance programme at Lancaster University in the 2020/21 academic year.

- **Award for Best Dissertation Results 2020/21**
  Highest dissertation mark on the MSc Quantitative Finance programme at Lancaster University in the 2020/21 academic year.

- **Lancaster Gold Award**
  Awarded for skills developed outside academia, including digital skills, charity work, work experience, and serving as a Student Academic Representative.
